title: pilosella fallacina
intro: Pilosella fallacina, also known as false mouse-ear hawk's-beard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family Asteraceae. It is native to Europe and Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Pilosella fallacina is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions.
